content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as recursive self-improving agents, where the memory system itself acts as an independent curiosity-driven agent that prioritizes which new experiences to encode, prune outdated information, and reorganize existing memory traces based on its own intrinsic reward for reducing the main task agent’s uncertainty. This creates a nested agent architecture where the memory subsystem actively learns to improve itself rather than passively storing data from the primary task agent.
